author: Dr. Wei Lin (Anatomic Pathology)
content:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT — GREATER BALTIMORE MEDICAL CENTER

Specimen: Gallbladder (cholecystectomy, 6/10/2027).
Clinical history: Perforated acute cholecystitis with peritonitis.

GROSS: Gallbladder with markedly thickened, dusky, friable wall and a focal full-thickness defect (perforation) at the fundus. Multiple mixed gallstones within the lumen. Purulent/bilious exudate on the serosal surface.

MICROSCOPIC: Transmural acute and chronic inflammation with extensive mucosal ulceration, hemorrhagic necrosis, and a focal area of transmural necrosis with perforation. Vascular congestion and acute serositis. No granulomas. No dysplasia. No invasive carcinoma. No porcelain (calcified) gallbladder.

DIAGNOSIS:
1. Gallbladder, cholecystectomy: ACUTE GANGRENOUS CHOLECYSTITIS with transmural necrosis and PERFORATION.
2. Cholelithiasis.
3. NEGATIVE for malignancy and dysplasia.

The findings confirm the clinical and intraoperative impression. No incidental neoplastic finding requiring additional oncologic follow-up.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Wei Lin, MD (Anatomic Pathology). 6/12/2027.
